stem: A product enters the manufacturing pipeline. The probability of passing raw material inspection is 82%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing machining is 71%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing assembly is between 76% and 93% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the product is shipped?

ground_truth.answer: P(shipped) ranges from 0.442 to 0.541 as the assembly pass rate varies from 76% to 93%. Point estimate: 0.489.

solution_steps: The product must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the assembly stage creates a 9.9 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the assembly rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
